name: Bevy Game Engine (Rust)

performance.notes: ECS architecture with parallelism; fast Rust native; GPU rendering via wgpu

agent_readiness.notes: Modern data-driven game engine in Rust. Entity-Component-System (ECS) architecture. Parallel systems by default. Cross-platform (Windows, macOS, Linux, WASM, iOS, Android). GPU rendering via wgpu (Metal, Vulkan, DX12). Hot reloading. 2D and 3D. Physics via plugins (Rapier). Rapid development cycles from Rust ecosystem. Fastest-growing Rust game engine. Not yet 1.0 but production-used. Growing plugin ecosystem (Bevy community).

gotchas: Pre-1.0 — breaking changes with each version
ECS paradigm is unfamiliar for traditional OOP game developers
Slower development velocity than Unity/Unreal for 3D content
Rust compile times slow development iteration
best_when: Rust native game development prioritizing performance
Cross-platform games including WASM browser deployment
Simulation systems benefiting from ECS parallelism
Developers who prefer Rust ecosystem over Unity/Godot
avoid_when: Large 3D AAA content creation (Unity/Unreal have better tooling)
summary: Bevy is a modern data-driven Rust game engine with ECS architecture. Parallel systems by default. Cross-platform including WASM. GPU rendering via wgpu. Fast-growing with strong community. Pre-1.0 but production-used. Best for Rust developers building games or simulations needing native performance and cross-platform deployment. Breaking changes expected until 1.0.
